what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600×900 HTML design composition with a centered title, three tinted statistic panels, a short centered body line, faint blueprint corner grids, a small circular mark, and a subdued hatched footer strip.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
The page is a single fixed-size .view canvas centered in the browser body.
Background uses layered warm gradients and color-mix with CSS custom properties.
Blueprint grid textures appear in the top-left and top-right corners with radial masks and low opacity.
Three statistic cards are laid out horizontally with warm-neutral surface variants and distinct figure colors.
The body copy is a single centered line: "Three measures, one direction".
Footer band markup contains a clock and momentum label, but a later correction block hides all footer children and reduces the band to a thin hatch.
CSS defines .subrow and .chip styles, but no matching subrow markup is present.
The circular "V" mark is positioned near the lower-right area and lifted by the correction block.
standouts: Fixed presentation-canvas layout with absolute positioning throughout.
Warm-neutral visual system with ochre, bronze, goldwash, charcoal, and blueprint accents.
Visible design-history comments label goals and a v2.1 correction inside the stylesheet.
Footer content remains in the DOM while being visually suppressed by later CSS.
conventions: Uses semantic-light HTML with visual meaning carried mostly by classes and CSS custom properties.
Uses class suffixes such as .s1, .s2, and .s3 for card surface variants.
Uses comments as design annotations for grid, rhythm, surface, footer, and correction decisions.
Breaks single-definition styling by redefining .gband and .mark later with !important overrides.
Relies on fixed pixel dimensions and absolute offsets rather than responsive layout.
concerns: Fixed 1600×900 sizing may not adapt to other viewport sizes.
The second .gband block overrides earlier footer styling with !important, which makes the final state harder to read from the first definition.
Hidden footer copy remains in the markup despite being visually suppressed.
Unused .subrow and .chip CSS increases file-local noise.
The external stylesheet path is absolute and required for the CSS variables to resolve.
notes: A fixed warm-toned presentation slide emphasizing three metrics, vertical spacing, faint blueprint texture, and a quiet corrected footer treatment.
questions: Should the hidden footer label remain in the DOM if the intended final footer is only a thin hatch?
Was the unused .subrow/.chip styling retained intentionally for a later variant?
Is the composition expected to stay fixed at 1600×900 or adapt responsively?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it implements a vertical-rhythm variation with tinted stats and a subdued footer correction.
intention_signals: It seems intended to refine a metrics slide into a calmer, warmer, vertically spaced composition while reducing the footer from an explanatory band to a subtle grounding texture.
novelty: The file carries an explicit correction layer that neutralizes earlier footer content without removing the original markup or first-pass CSS.
contribution: Contributes a warm fixed-canvas metric-slide substrate with faint blueprint corner texture, three tinted statistic panels, centered sparse copy, and a thin hatched footer treatment that a fused system could retain as a layout and atmosphere pattern.
summary_for_embedding: Fixed 1600×900 HTML/CSS presentation composition with warm gradient background, blueprint corner grids, centered title/body text, three tinted statistic cards, lower-right V mark, and a corrected thin hatched footer.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for exploring a quiet vertical-rhythm metrics layout with warm-neutral stat surfaces and reduced footer emphasis.
